seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Check block positions now
print("Right panel blocks now:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in 'RbN' and n['boundary'][0][1] >= 40:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {n['boundary'][0][0]}-{n['boundary'][2][0]}, cols {n['boundary'][0][1]}-{n['boundary'][2][1]}")

# Check agent position
print("\nAgent now:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 50 and n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  rows {n['boundary'][0][0]}-{n['boundary'][6][0]}")

# The blocks moved back to original positions when agent moved UP
# This confirms: blocks follow agent vertically
# Now I need to figure out what the goal is
# Bottom panel shows: R, N, b
# Right panel shows: R, b, N (top to bottom)

# Check marker positions
print("\nLeft markers:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in 'gG' and n['boundary'][0][1] < 15 and 15 < n['boundary'][0][0] < 50:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {n['boundary'][0][0]}-{n['boundary'][2][0]}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
